items.title: You don’t have to be rich to be financially independent. Here’s how to take control of your money.
items.description: Relying on a high salary or a hot stock is a dangerous trap. Why the most secure people plan for sudden crisis.

items.title: Stock pickers know they can’t outsmart the market. That doesn’t stop them from trying.
items.description: How to satisfy your inner trader without derailing your long-term investment goals.

items.title: Billionaires are loading up on these superior stocks. Why it pays to follow their money.
items.description: The second quarter was a six-year best for U.S. stocks — here are the three sectors poised to lead the next leg higher.

items.title: Micron and other chip stocks feel the pain of imported volatility — blame SK Hynix
items.description: The SOX chip index was suffering a unanimous selloff, after SK Hynix’s stock had its worst day in 18 years in South Korea trading.

items.title: SpaceX’s stock threatens to fall below the IPO price. Do investors face a ‘crisis’ if it does?
items.description: The company has shed more than $800 billion in market value from its $2.67 trillion peak in less than a month.

items.title: Global oil prices top $83 a barrel, logging biggest jump in 6 years after Trump reimposes Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.description: The week is kicking off with fresh spikes for oil prices after President Donald Trump reimposed the naval blockage on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: AI-related debt jumped 99% over the past year. It’s a ‘shock to the system’ for investors.
items.description: A deluge of hyperscaler debt creates risk that investors will run up against concentration limits in their portfolios from a single company or industry.

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: Meta and Amazon are leading a trillion-dollar Big Tech spending spree
items.description: As the cost of AI infrastructure continues to increase, Morgan Stanley says Big Tech capital expenditures are on track to hit new records.

items.title: My wife’s 2011 Ford Fiesta was totaled. Should we accept a $2,000 insurance settlement or give up the car for $2,700?
items.description: “The hood is bent, the radiator is cracked, and the front bumper is destroyed.”
